title: AutoGen Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using AutoGen, covering API, CLI, and advanced features.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative content that requires diverse outputs.

commands.description: Uses the GPT-4o model with streaming output.
commands.scenario: When building a real-time chatbot that requires immediate responses.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the model to reason through problems methodically.
commands.scenario: When solving complex engineering problems that require logical reasoning.

commands.description: Limits the response length to save on processing costs.
commands.scenario: When generating concise summaries or short answers.

commands.description: Enables detailed logging for debugging purposes.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting API calls to understand request and response details.

commands.command: Use markdown format for the response.
commands.description: Instructs the model to format the output in markdown.
commands.scenario: When generating content for web pages or documentation.

commands.description: Specifies the API key for authentication.
commands.scenario: When making secure API requests to the AutoGen service.

commands.description: Sets the size of the context window for the model.
commands.scenario: When handling large documents or extensive conversations.

commands.command: Use examples to clarify your request.
commands.description: Provides examples to guide the model's understanding of the task.
commands.scenario: When the task is complex and requires specific guidance.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent hanging.
commands.scenario: When integrating into systems with strict performance requirements.

commands.description: Reduces the likelihood of repeating the same line verbatim.
commands.scenario: When generating varied text in creative writing applications.

commands.description: Instructs the model to use bullet points for list formatting.
commands.scenario: When creating structured content like reports or presentations.

commands.description: Retries failed API calls up to three times.
commands.scenario: When ensuring reliability in network communication.

commands.description: Decorator used to bind a function to an agent's LLM capabilities, enabling seamless tool-calling workflows.
Configures the orchestration logic for multi-agent conversations, allowing the manager to dynamically select the next speaker.
Sets the agent to run autonomously without waiting for user intervention, essential for automated testing and CI/CD pipelines.
Defines a dedicated sandbox directory for agent-generated code execution to prevent file system clutter and improve security.

commands.command: system_message='You are a critic. Identify logical fallacies in the previous response.'
commands.description: Implements a 'Critic-Agent' pattern to improve output accuracy through adversarial multi-agent prompting.
Enforces a strict sequential turn-taking order among agents, preventing a single agent from dominating the conversation loop.
A decorator used to bind a Python function to an agent, allowing the LLM to trigger local code execution via tool calling.
Enables semantic caching to reduce API costs and latency by reusing responses for identical or highly similar prompts.
Manually injects a message into an agent's context window, useful for programmatic state injection or simulating external events.

commands.command: Use 'Self-Correction' pattern: 'Review your previous code for syntax errors and re-run it if necessary.'
commands.description: A prompt engineering technique to trigger an agent's internal feedback loop within a single turn to reduce execution failures.
